workflow.name: Generate GitLab release notes from Linear issues with Claude Opus
workflow.description:
## 📝 Release Note Helper

Triggered by a GitLab MR webhook, this workflow automatically assists your team in writing **customer-facing release notes** by combining Linear issue data with Claude AI.

Apply the `rn-release-n8n` label to any release note MR in your docs repository to trigger it.

## How it works

1. **Version detection** — reads your release RSS feed to find the last published version, then fetches all matching Linear version labels created since then to determine the version range automatically
2. **Issue collection** — queries Linear for all completed issues in that version range that have Zendesk tickets, Slack links, or custom labels (`Customer request`, `Release note public`) attached
3. **Ticket summary** — posts a structured list of all relevant issues to the MR as a comment
4. **AI draft** — sends issue details to Claude, which generates customer-facing changelog entries grouped into `### Enhancements` and `### Fixes`, posted as a second MR comment
5. **Done label** — adds `rn-done` to the MR when complete to prevent re-runs

## Setup

1. Configure a **GitLab webhook** on your docs repo pointing to this workflow's URL (Merge Request events)
2. Create two labels on your GitLab repo: `rn-release-n8n` (to trigger) and `rn-done` (auto-applied on completion)
3. Update the **RSS Read** node URL to your release RSS feed
4. Replace `YOUR_PROJECT_ID` in all GitLab API nodes with your docs project ID
5. Replace `YOUR_WORKSPACE` in the Code nodes with your Linear workspace slug
6. Connect **Linear API**, **GitLab API**, and **Anthropic API** credentials

## Notes

- Versioning assumes a `vX.Y` Linear label convention — adapt the **Format labels** node for your own scheme
- The AI prompt in **Message a model** is ready to use but can be customised to match your tone and changelog format
- Issues are filtered to those with Zendesk, Slack attachments, or your custom labels — adjust in **Set Params**

workflow.name: Release Note Helper (Linear + GitLab + Claude AI)

parameters.content:
## Release Note Helper (Linear + GitLab + Claude AI)

### How it works

1. Triggers on a GitLab merge request (MR) event to start the process.
2. Checks existing MR conditions to determine if a release label is present.
3. Retrieves and formats labels from a Linear RSS feed.
4. Based on label presence, gathers Linear issues to generate summaries and suggestions.
5. Applies AI to enhance MR summaries and updates the MR with suggestions and labels.
6. Completes the MR process with additional notes if needed.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Configure GitLab credentials for API access.
- [ ] Set up Linear API credentials for issue retrieval.
- [ ] Ensure access to the AI model service for generating summaries.

### Customization

Adjust conditions and API endpoints for different GitLab projects or Linear workspaces.

## Trigger and initial condition

Starts the workflow when a merge request is opened or updated in GitLab, then checks if the MR already contains release note inputs.

## Check and read RSS for labels

Evaluates if a release label exists and reads relevant RSS feed to get initial labels.

## Retrieve and process Linear labels

Fetches labels from Linear, extracts specific versions, and sets parameters for further processing.

## Get Linear issues and manage response

Retrieves Linear issues and decides the subsequent action, including generating summaries or adding notes if issues are absent.

## Generate and add high level summary

Creates a high-level summary and adds it to the GitLab MR.

## AI suggestions and issue management

Generates AI-driven suggestions and applies labels, finalizing MR modifications with notes if necessary.

nodes.name: Post No Issues Note to GitLab

parameters.name: body
parameters.value: =There are no recent Linear issues with Zendesk tickets attached for ``{{ $('Parse Linear Version Labels').item.json.versionRange }}`` or with the following labels: ``{{ $('Parse Linear Version Labels').item.json.custom_labels }}``.

parameters.url: =https://gitlab.example.com/api/v4/projects/YOUR_PROJECT_ID/merge_requests/{{ $('When GitLab MR Event Occurs').first().json.body.object_attributes.iid }}/discussions/{{ $('Post Summary to GitLab MR').first().json.id}}/notes

parameters.name: body
parameters.value:
=## 🤖 Suggestion from Claude AI ✨ to be verified:

{{ $json.content[0].text }}

values.content:
=You are a technical writer generating concise, customer-facing release notes.

<data>
{{ $json.markdown }}
</data>

<task>
For each issue in the data above, write a single-line release note entry suitable for a public changelog.
</task>

<formatting_instructions>
- Group entries into two sections:
  - `### Enhancements` — new features, improvements, or UX changes
  - `### Fixes` — bugs, regressions, or reliability issues
- Each entry must:
  - Start with `- [ ] `
  - Begin with the **Feature Area** in bold (e.g. `**API**:`, `**Dashboard**:`)
  - Be written in clear, customer-friendly language — no internal jargon
  - End with the linked Linear issue ID: `**[ISSUE-123](https://linear.app/YOUR_WORKSPACE/issue/ISSUE-123)**`
- Group multiple entries under the same Feature Area using nested bullets
- Do not include emojis
- Use a professional tone appropriate for an external changelog
</formatting_instructions>

<example>
### Enhancements
- [ ] **API**: Added support for filtering by key/value pairs, improving search flexibility. **[ISSUE-101](https://linear.app/YOUR_WORKSPACE/issue/ISSUE-101)**
- [ ] **Dashboard**:
  - [ ] Introduced a new summary view for faster navigation. **[ISSUE-102](https://linear.app/YOUR_WORKSPACE/issue/ISSUE-102)**
  - [ ] Export to CSV now includes all custom fields. **[ISSUE-103](https://linear.app/YOUR_WORKSPACE/issue/ISSUE-103)**

### Fixes
- [ ] **Notifications**: Fixed an issue where alerts were sent to deactivated users. **[ISSUE-104](https://linear.app/YOUR_WORKSPACE/issue/ISSUE-104)**
</example>

<notes>
Use the issue title, description, and labels from the data to craft accurate summaries.
If the feature area is unclear, infer it from the issue's team or labels.
If the title is vague, use the description to understand the user impact.
Keep each entry concise and focused on what changed for the user.
</notes>

main.node: Post No Issues Note to GitLab

user.name: Romain Jouhannet
user.bio: Technical Product Manager with a background in software development. Experienced in launching products, leading teams, and creating API-first solutions. Skilled in SaaS, Self-Hosted products, major public clouds, and automation, with a focus on simplifying complex technical concepts for enterprise clients.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
